title: 3rd Infantry Division Ravenna

infobox.value: [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II")  * [Italian invasion of France](/wiki/Italian_invasion_of_France "Italian invasion of France") * [Invasion of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Invasion_of_Yugoslavia "Invasion of Yugoslavia") * [Eastern front](/wiki/Eastern_Front_(World_War_II) "Eastern Front (World War II)") * [Operation Little Saturn](/wiki/Operation_Little_Saturn "Operation Little Saturn")

([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *3ª Divisione di fanteria "Ravenna"*) was an [infantry](/wiki/Infantry "Infantry") [division](/wiki/Division_(military) "Division (military)") of the [Royal Italian Army](/wiki/Royal_Italian_Army "Royal Italian Army") during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"). The Ravenna was classified as a mountain infantry division, which meant that the division's artillery was moved by pack mules instead of the horse-drawn carriages of line infantry divisions. Italy's real [mountain warfare](/wiki/Mountain_warfare "Mountain warfare") divisions were the six alpine divisions manned by [Alpini](/wiki/Alpini "Alpini") mountain troops. The Ravenna was named for the ancient city of [Ravenna](/wiki/Ravenna "Ravenna") and based in the city of [Alessandria](/wiki/Alessandria "Alessandria"). Its two infantry regiments were based in Alessandria (37th) and [Alba](/wiki/Alba,_Piedmont "Alba, Piedmont") (38th), respectively Alessandria and [Tortona](/wiki/Tortona "Tortona") from 1939, with the division's artillery regiment also based in Alessandria.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [3rd Infantry Division Ravenna](/w/index.php?title=3rd_Infantry_Division_Ravenna&redirect=no "3rd Infantry Division Ravenna"))

Military unit

The **3rd Infantry Division "Ravenna"** ([Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"): *3ª Divisione di fanteria "Ravenna"*) was an [infantry](/wiki/Infantry "Infantry") [division](/wiki/Division_(military) "Division (military)") of the [Royal Italian Army](/wiki/Royal_Italian_Army "Royal Italian Army") during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"). The Ravenna was classified as a mountain infantry division, which meant that the division's artillery was moved by pack mules instead of the horse-drawn carriages of line infantry divisions. Italy's real [mountain warfare](/wiki/Mountain_warfare "Mountain warfare") divisions were the six alpine divisions manned by [Alpini](/wiki/Alpini "Alpini") mountain troops. The Ravenna was named for the ancient city of [Ravenna](/wiki/Ravenna "Ravenna") and based in the city of [Alessandria](/wiki/Alessandria "Alessandria"). Its two infantry regiments were based in Alessandria (37th) and [Alba](/wiki/Alba,_Piedmont "Alba, Piedmont") (38th), respectively Alessandria and [Tortona](/wiki/Tortona "Tortona") from 1939, with the division's artillery regiment also based in Alessandria.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3rd_Infantry_Division_%22Ravenna%22&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The division's lineage begins with the XI Brigade established on 24 June 1859 with the 19th and 20th infantry regiments of the Army of the [United Provinces of Central Italy](/wiki/United_Provinces_of_Central_Italy "United Provinces of Central Italy"). On 16 September 1859 the brigade received the name "Ravenna". On 25 March 1860 the Brigade "Ravenna" entered the [Royal Sardinian Army](/wiki/Royal_Sardinian_Army "Royal Sardinian Army") three days after the [Kingdom of Sardinia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Sardinia "Kingdom of Sardinia") had annexed the United Provinces of Central Italy. Already before entering the Royal Sardinian Army the brigade's two infantry regiments had been renumbered on 30 December 1859 as 37th Infantry Regiment and 38th Infantry Regiment.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### World War I

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3rd_Infantry_Division_%22Ravenna%22&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: World War I")]

The brigade fought on the [Italian front](/wiki/Italian_front_(World_War_I) "Italian front (World War I)") in [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I"). On 13 October 1926 the brigade was disbanded and its two regiments were reassigned: the [37th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna"](/wiki/37th_Infantry_Regiment_%22Ravenna%22 "37th Infantry Regiment \"Ravenna\"") to the III Infantry Brigade and the 38th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna" to the [IV Infantry Brigade](/wiki/4th_Infantry_Division_%22Livorno%22 "4th Infantry Division \"Livorno\""). The III Infantry Brigade, the former Brigade "Forlì", also included the 43rd Infantry Regiment "Forlì" and the 44th Infantry Regiment "Forlì". The brigade was the infantry component of the 3rd Territorial Division of Alessandria, which also included the [11th Artillery Regiment](/wiki/11th_Artillery_Regiment_%22Ravenna%22 "11th Artillery Regiment \"Ravenna\"").[[3]](#cite_note-Regio_Esercito-3)[[4]](#cite_note-37°_"Ravenna"-4)[[5]](#cite_note-38°_"Ravenna"-5)

In 1930 the division exchanged the 44th Infantry Regiment "Forlì" for the 38th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna" with the [4th Territorial Division of Cuneo](/wiki/4th_Infantry_Division_%22Livorno%22 "4th Infantry Division \"Livorno\""). In 1935 the division changed its name to 3rd Infantry Division "[Monferrato](/wiki/Monferrato "Monferrato")". On 1 April 1934 the division exchanged the 38th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna" for [30th Infantry Regiment "Pisa"](/wiki/30th_Infantry_Regiment_%22Pisa%22 "30th Infantry Regiment \"Pisa\"") with the [26th Infantry Division "Assietta"](/wiki/26th_Infantry_Division_%22Assietta%22 "26th Infantry Division \"Assietta\""). On 25 March 1939 the 38th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna" returned to the division and the 30th Infantry Regiment "Pisa" was transferred back to the Assietta division. On 31 March 1939 the 43rd Infantry Regiment "Forlì" was transferred to the newly activated [36th Infantry Division "Forlì"](/wiki/36th_Infantry_Division_%22Forl%C3%AC%22 "36th Infantry Division \"Forlì\"") and on the same date the III Infantry Brigade was dissolved and the two remaining infantry regiments came under direct command of the division, which changed its name to 3rd Infantry Division "Ravenna".[[3]](#cite_note-Regio_Esercito-3)

### World War II

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3rd_Infantry_Division_%22Ravenna%22&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: World War II")]

During the [Italian invasion of France](/wiki/Italian_invasion_of_France "Italian invasion of France") in June 1940 the Ravenna was part of [III Army Corps](/wiki/III_Army_Corps_(Italy) "III Army Corps (Italy)") of [1st Army](/wiki/1st_Army_(Italy) "1st Army (Italy)").[[6]](#cite_note-6) At the campaigns conclusions on 24 June 1940 it had reached the village of [Fontan](/wiki/Fontan_(village) "Fontan (village)").

In preparation for the [Invasion of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Invasion_of_Yugoslavia "Invasion of Yugoslavia") the Ravenna was assigned to [XI Army Corps](/wiki/XI_Army_Corps_(Italy) "XI Army Corps (Italy)") and transferred to the Italian-Yugoslav border near [Kobarid](/wiki/Kobarid "Kobarid") and [Most na Soči](/wiki/Most_na_So%C4%8Di "Most na Soči") in early April 1941.[[7]](#cite_note-7) After the Yugoslav surrender the division performed mop-up operations in [Škofja Loka](/wiki/%C5%A0kofja_Loka "Škofja Loka") from 14 April 1941 until 17 April 1941, before handing the city over to German forces. Next mop-up operations were undertaken near [Mirna](/wiki/Mirna_(settlement) "Mirna (settlement)") village. In early May 1941 the Ravenna was transferred to [Pivka](/wiki/Pivka "Pivka") and then returned to [Alessandria](/wiki/Alessandria "Alessandria"), where it remained until 1942.[[3]](#cite_note-Regio_Esercito-3)

#### Eastern Front

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3rd_Infantry_Division_%22Ravenna%22&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Eastern Front")]

The division was one of ten Italian divisions, which served on the [Eastern Front](/wiki/Eastern_Front_(World_War_II) "Eastern Front (World War II)") as part of the [Italian Army in Russia](/wiki/Italian_Army_in_Russia "Italian Army in Russia"). The Ravenna division received orders to move from [Piedmont](/wiki/Piedmont "Piedmont") to Eastern Ukraine in June 1942. Before leaving Italy the Ravenna exchanged artillery regiments with the [104th Infantry Division "Mantova"](/wiki/104th_Infantry_Division_%22Mantova%22 "104th Infantry Division \"Mantova\""), as the Mantova's [121st Motorized Artillery Regiment](/wiki/121st_Motorized_Artillery_Regiment_(Italy) "121st Motorized Artillery Regiment (Italy)") was equipped with modern [75/18 Mod. 34](/wiki/Obice_da_75/18_modello_34 "Obice da 75/18 modello 34") howitzers, which were considered to be of better use in the [Soviet Union](/wiki/Soviet_Union "Soviet Union") than the [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I") vintage [75/27 Mod. 11](/wiki/Cannone_da_75/27_modello_11 "Cannone da 75/27 modello 11") [field guns](/wiki/Field_gun "Field gun") of the Ravenna's 11th Artillery Regiment "Ravenna". The Ravenna arrived in Stalino (today [Donetsk](/wiki/Donetsk "Donetsk")) in [Eastern Ukraine](/wiki/Eastern_Ukraine "Eastern Ukraine") in July 1942 and was assigned to the Italian [XXXV Army Corps](/wiki/XXXV_Army_Corps_(Italy) "XXXV Army Corps (Italy)"). They moved from Stalino to Voroshilovgrad (today [Luhansk](/wiki/Luhansk "Luhansk")) on 25 July and then proceeded to the [Don](/wiki/Don_(river) "Don (river)") river, where the division took up defensive positions between [Verhny Mamon](https://ru.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D0%92%D0%B5%D1%80%D1%85%D0%BD%D0%B8%D0%B9_%D0%9C%D0%B0%D0%BC%D0%BE%D0%BD "ru:Верхний Мамон") and [Boguchar](/wiki/Boguchar "Boguchar"). In this sector the Ravenna defeated series of Soviet assault between 20 August and 1 September 1942. On 11 September the Soviet forces renewed their attacks focusing on Solonets, south of [Voronezh](/wiki/Voronezh "Voronezh"). Afterwards the front calmed until by mid-November 1942, German intelligence spotted the massing of the Soviet 5th Tank Army across the Don. A German officer attached to the [5th Infantry Division "Cosseria"](/wiki/5th_Infantry_Division_%22Cosseria%22 "5th Infantry Division \"Cosseria\"") wrote, that the morale of the division and the neighbouring Ravenna was confident considering all the difficulties.

#### Operation Little Saturn

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3rd_Infantry_Division_%22Ravenna%22&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Operation Little Saturn")]

On 11 December 1942 Soviet forces began [Operation Little Saturn](/wiki/Operation_Little_Saturn "Operation Little Saturn"). The Ravenna division was attacked from either end of the '[pocket](/wiki/Pocket_(military) "Pocket (military)")' below the Don river held by the Soviets since their August offensive. The division's commander, [Major general](/wiki/Major_general "Major general") Francesco Du Pont, asked for a diversionary attack by the nearest [Alpini](/wiki/Alpini "Alpini") division. General [Italo Gariboldi](/wiki/Italo_Gariboldi "Italo Gariboldi"), Commander of the [8th Army](/wiki/8th_Army_(Italy) "8th Army (Italy)"), refused because he thought the enemy facing it was too strong and there was too little time. Nor did he deploy the German [27th Panzer Division](/wiki/27th_Panzer_Division_(Wehrmacht) "27th Panzer Division (Wehrmacht)"), which was under his command. The Ravenna destroyed 70 of the 200 Soviet tanks that attacked it, and Du Pont was awarded an [Iron Cross](/wiki/Iron_Cross "Iron Cross") First Class in the field by the Germans.

In the following days, however, the intense enemy pressure led to some units of the Ravenna near [Verhny Mamon](https://ru.wikipedia.org/wiki/%D0%92%D0%B5%D1%80%D1%85%D0%BD%D0%B8%D0%B9_%D0%9C%D0%B0%D0%BC%D0%BE%D0%BD "ru:Верхний Мамон") to fall back, thus opening a gap in the Axis' frontline.

By 17 December 1942 Soviet forces reached [Chertkovo](/wiki/Chertkovo,_Rostov_Oblast "Chertkovo, Rostov Oblast") in the rear of Axis forces. The Soviet [1st Guards Army](/wiki/Soviet_First_Guards_Army "Soviet First Guards Army") and the [3rd Guards Army](/wiki/Soviet_Third_Guards_Army "Soviet Third Guards Army") attacked from the north, attempting to encircle 130,000 soldiers of the Italian Army in Russia and advancing to [Millerovo](/wiki/Millerovo "Millerovo").[[3]](#cite_note-Regio_Esercito-3)

On 17 December the bulk of the Ravenna had retreated to Voroshilovgrad, where the division reorganized itself and then returned to the front defending the bridges over the [Donets](/wiki/Donets "Donets") river at Veselaya Gora-[Stanytsia Luhanska](/wiki/Stanytsia_Luhanska "Stanytsia Luhanska") 22–30 December. During the retreat elements of the Ravenna had been separated from the division and been surrounded on 23 December 1942 at [Chertkovo](/wiki/Chertkovo,_Rostov_Oblast "Chertkovo, Rostov Oblast"), where they, together with other German and Italian units, resisted repeated Soviet attacks. From 1–6 January 1943 the Ravenna was subordinated the German Armeegruppe [Fretter-Pico](/wiki/Maximilian_Fretter-Pico "Maximilian Fretter-Pico") and was defending the western bank of the Donets river. On 15 January the Ravenna tried and succeed to break open the encirclement at Chertkovo.[[3]](#cite_note-Regio_Esercito-3)

After Soviet forces had pierced the Donets front on 24 January the Ravenna was forced to retreat under pressure from Soviet armored spearheads. On 17 January the division's remnants reached [Bilovodsk](/wiki/Bilovodsk "Bilovodsk") and were taken out of the frontline. In April 1943 the remnants of the division were transferred back to Italy, where the division was being reformed in [Tuscany](/wiki/Tuscany "Tuscany"). After the announcement of the [Armistice of Cassibile](/wiki/Armistice_of_Cassibile "Armistice of Cassibile") on 8 September 1943 the division was disbanded by the [invading German forces](/wiki/Operation_Achse "Operation Achse").[[3]](#cite_note-Regio_Esercito-3)

## Organization

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3rd_Infantry_Division_%22Ravenna%22&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Organization")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/49/CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_fanteria_038.png/250px-CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_fanteria_038.png)](/wiki/File:CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_fanteria_038.png)

Coat of Arms of the 38th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna", 1939

When the division was deployed to the Soviet Union it consisted of the following units:[[8]](#cite_note-8)

* [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/44/3a_Divisione_Fanteria_Ravenna.png/40px-3a_Divisione_Fanteria_Ravenna.png)](/wiki/File:3a_Divisione_Fanteria_Ravenna.png) **3rd Infantry Division "Ravenna"**, in [Alessandria](/wiki/Alessandria "Alessandria")[[3]](#cite_note-Regio_Esercito-3)[[9]](#cite_note-Ufficio_Storico-9)
  + [37th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna"](/wiki/37th_Infantry_Regiment_%22Ravenna%22 "37th Infantry Regiment \"Ravenna\""), in Alessandria[[4]](#cite_note-37°_"Ravenna"-4)
    - Command Company
    - 3x Fusilier battalions
    - Support Weapons Company ([65/17](/wiki/Cannone_da_65/17_modello_13 "Cannone da 65/17 modello 13") [infantry support guns](/wiki/Infantry_support_gun "Infantry support gun"))
    - Mortar Company ([81mm mod. 35](/wiki/81/14_Model_35_Mortar "81/14 Model 35 Mortar") [mortars](/wiki/Mortar_(weapon) "Mortar (weapon)"))
  + 38th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna", in [Tortona](/wiki/Tortona "Tortona") (in [Alba](/wiki/Alba,_Piedmont "Alba, Piedmont") 1923–1939)[[5]](#cite_note-38°_"Ravenna"-5)
    - Command Company
    - 3x Fusilier battalions
    - Support Weapons Company (65/17 infantry support guns)
    - Mortar Company (81mm mod. 35 mortars)
  + [11th Artillery Regiment "Ravenna"](/wiki/11th_Artillery_Regiment_%22Ravenna%22 "11th Artillery Regiment \"Ravenna\""), in [Alessandria](/wiki/Alessandria "Alessandria") (transferred to the [104th Infantry Division "Mantova"](/wiki/104th_Infantry_Division_%22Mantova%22 "104th Infantry Division \"Mantova\"") in June 1942)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
    - Command Unit
    - I Group ([100/17 mod. 14](/wiki/10_cm_M._14_Feldhaubitze "10 cm M. 14 Feldhaubitze") howitzers; re-equipped with [100/22 mod. 14/19](/wiki/Skoda_houfnice_vz_14 "Skoda houfnice vz 14") howitzers in early 1943)
    - II Group ([75/13 mod. 15](/wiki/Skoda_75_mm_Model_15 "Skoda 75 mm Model 15") [mountain guns](/wiki/Mountain_gun "Mountain gun"))
    - III Group (75/13 mod. 15 mountain guns; transferred in September 1940 to the 49th Artillery Regiment "Parma" in exchange for a group with [75/27 mod. 06](/wiki/Cannone_da_75/27_modello_06 "Cannone da 75/27 modello 06") [field guns](/wiki/Field_gun "Field gun"))
    - 1× Anti-aircraft battery ([20/65 mod. 35](/wiki/Breda_Model_35 "Breda Model 35") [anti-aircraft guns](/wiki/Anti-aircraft_gun "Anti-aircraft gun"))
    - Ammunition and Supply Unit
  + [121st Motorized Artillery Regiment](/wiki/121st_Anti-aircraft_Artillery_Regiment_%22Ravenna%22 "121st Anti-aircraft Artillery Regiment \"Ravenna\"") (transferred from the 104th Infantry Division "Mantova" in June 1942)[[11]](#cite_note-11)
    - Command Unit
    - I Group ([75/18 mod. 35](/wiki/Obice_da_75/18_modello_34 "Obice da 75/18 modello 34") howitzers)
    - II Group (75/18 mod. 35 howitzers)
    - XXVIII Group ([105/28](/w/index.php?title=Cannone_da_105/28&action=edit&redlink=1 "Cannone da 105/28 (page does not exist)") cannons)
    - 51st Anti-aircraft Battery ([20/65 mod. 35](/wiki/Breda_Model_35 "Breda Model 35") anti-aircraft guns; attached to the regiment in the Soviet Union)
    - 303rd Anti-aircraft Battery (20/65 mod. 35 anti-aircraft guns)
    - Ammunition and Supply Unit
  + III Anti-tank Battalion (formed during the deployment to the Eastern Front)
    - 3rd Anti-tank Company ([47/32](/wiki/Cannone_da_47/32 "Cannone da 47/32") anti-tank guns)
    - 71st Anti-tank Battery ([75/39](/wiki/7.5_cm_Pak_97/38 "7.5 cm Pak 97/38") anti-tank guns; attached during the deployment in the Soviet Union)
    - 154th Anti-tank Company (47/32 anti-tank guns; transferred from the [154th Infantry Division "Murge"](/wiki/154th_Infantry_Division_%22Murge%22 "154th Infantry Division \"Murge\"") for the deployment in the Soviet Union)
  + III Mortar Battalion (81mm mod. 35 mortars)
  + 3rd Telegraph and Radio Operators Company
  + 18th Engineer Company
  + 18th Medical Section
    - 16th Field Hospital
    - 201st Field Hospital
    - 202nd Field Hospital
    - 438th Field Hospital
    - 37th Surgical Unit
  + 7th Supply Section (expanded to 7th Supply Unit for the deployment to the Soviet Union)
  + 3rd Truck Section
  + 128th Transport Section
  + 247th Transport Section
  + 49th Bakers Section
  + 7th [Carabinieri](/wiki/Carabinieri "Carabinieri") Section
  + 8th Carabinieri Section
  + 3rd Infantry Division Command Transport Squad
  + 53rd Field Post Office

Attached during the [invasion of France](/wiki/Italian_invasion_of_France "Italian invasion of France") in 1940:[[9]](#cite_note-Ufficio_Storico-9)

* [Alpini](/wiki/Alpini "Alpini") Battalion "Ceva", from the [1st Alpini Regiment](/wiki/1st_Alpini_Regiment "1st Alpini Regiment")/ [4th Alpine Division "Cuneense"](/wiki/4th_Alpine_Division_%22Cuneense%22 "4th Alpine Division \"Cuneense\"")
* V [CC.NN.](/wiki/CC.NN. "CC.NN.") Battalion

Attached from late 1940 to April 1942:[[9]](#cite_note-Ufficio_Storico-9)

* 5th [CC.NN.](/wiki/CC.NN. "CC.NN.") Legion "Valle Scrivia"
  + Command Company
  + V CC.NN. Battalion
  + XXXIV CC.NN. Battalion
  + 5th CC.NN. Machine Gun Company

## Military honors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3rd_Infantry_Division_%22Ravenna%22&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Military honors")]

For their conduct during the [campaign in the Soviet Union](/wiki/Italian_participation_in_the_Eastern_Front "Italian participation in the Eastern Front") the [President of Italy](/wiki/President_of_Italy "President of Italy") awarded on 31 December 1947 to the two infantry regiments of the 3rd Infantry Division "Ravenna" Italy's highest military honor, the [Gold Medal of Military Valor](/wiki/Gold_Medal_of_Military_Valor "Gold Medal of Military Valor").

* [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7c/Valor_militare_gold_medal_BAR.svg/60px-Valor_militare_gold_medal_BAR.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Valor_militare_gold_medal_BAR.svg) 37th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna" on 31 December 1947[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7c/Valor_militare_gold_medal_BAR.svg/60px-Valor_militare_gold_medal_BAR.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Valor_militare_gold_medal_BAR.svg) 38th Infantry Regiment "Ravenna" on 31 December 1947[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Commanding officers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=3rd_Infantry_Division_%22Ravenna%22&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Commanding officers")]

The division's commanding officers were:[[3]](#cite_note-Regio_Esercito-3)[[9]](#cite_note-Ufficio_Storico-9)

* [Generale di Divisione](/wiki/Military_ranks_of_the_Kingdom_of_Italy#Ranks_during_the_Second_World_War "Military ranks of the Kingdom of Italy") [Matteo Roux](/wiki/Matteo_Roux "Matteo Roux") (1939 - 15 August 1939)
* Generale di Divisione [Edoardo Nebbia](/w/index.php?title=Edoardo_Nebbia&action=edit&redlink=1 "Edoardo Nebbia (page does not exist)") (16 August 1939 - 3 October 1942)
* Generale di Divisione [Francesco Du Pont](/w/index.php?title=Francesco_Du_Pont&action=edit&redlink=1 "Francesco Du Pont (page does not exist)") (4 October 1942 - 8 September 1943)
